seoTitle: How to Create a Request Type in Jira Service Management
metaDescription: Add a new request type in Jira Service Management so customers or employees can submit the right kind of ticket through your portal.
intro: Jira Service Management relies on request types to shape what your customer portal looks like and what information gets collected upfront. This guide covers creating a new request type from scratch.

steps.heading: Open your service project settings
steps.body: Go to the project settings for your service desk, where request types are managed.

steps.heading: Go to the request types area
steps.body: Find the section listing existing request types available in the customer portal.

steps.heading: Add a new request type
steps.body: Create a new type and choose the underlying issue type it maps to, such as Task or Incident.

steps.heading: Customize the request form
steps.body: Add or reorder the fields customers will fill in, and write clear labels so the form is easy to understand.

steps.heading: Publish it to the portal
steps.body: Make the request type visible in the customer portal and assign it to the right portal group.

faq.question: Can I hide a request type from certain customers?
faq.answer: Yes, request types can be restricted to specific customer groups or made visible to everyone using the portal.

faq.question: What's the difference between a request type and an issue type?
faq.answer: The issue type is the underlying Jira data structure, while the request type is the customer-facing form and name shown in the portal.

faq.question: Can I edit a request type after it's published?
faq.answer: Yes, you can update fields, descriptions, and visibility on a request type at any time without losing existing tickets.

faq.question: Do request types support conditional fields?
faq.answer: Some setups allow dynamic forms, though basic request types show a fixed set of fields to every requester.
